author: jtraglia
content: Hey <@402080271563751435> just want to quickly mention that optimistic _might_ be deprecated in Gloas. I'm so behind on this discussion though, so I could be wrong. But this is something to keep in mind. See: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1496606725422190652

author: jtraglia
content: Hmm maybe not deprecated. I don't really know the status. 😅

author: jtraglia
content:
Also, please update the PR title to be a sentence in the imperative mood. The things I look for in a title:

> Titles must be written in the imperative mood. Titles must not have component prefixes like the "conventional commit" style. Titles should use sentence case, not title case. Titles must be fewer than 72 characters long. Titles should not be too vague. Words that are clearly code (functions, classes, etc) must be wrapped in backticks. There must be no terminal punctuation and ideally no punctuation at all. Titles must not contain double spaces, leading whitespace, or trailing whitespace.

author: jtraglia
content: ~~And no need to mention EIP-8025 in the title 🙂~~ Actually nevermind. This doesn't touch eip8025 specs and therefore doesn't have the label.

author: taulepton_
content:
Thanks for flagging this. It is certainly of interest, not only for EIP8025 but also for EIP8142. 

However, I don't interpret this a deprecation of optimistic sync but more so a modification of the engineAPI's shape / execution request commitments to support decoupled EL/CL sync.

I'll be sure to enter the conversation on this front as we will need to ensure that all unfinalized blocks are sent to the proof engine (as the proof engine has no alternative network to sync from). I'll prepare a small contribution but I think it can broadly be summarised by "if the node client is operating a proof engine then range sync (bypassing the proof engine) should not be used to sync above the finalized head" however I need to investigate the implications further before I have strong conviction in this statement.

author: taulepton_
content: Do you have reference to this rule (and others). I wonder if we can add them to AGENTS.md?

author: jtraglia
content:
This text comes from this PR:
* https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5156
I'm working towards making AGENTS.md smaller.

author: jtraglia
content: Not sure an agent would pick this up when making PRs though.

author: jtraglia
content: Might need to make another skill for that.

author: jtraglia
content: I will add a `commit` skill to our todo list.

author: taulepton_
content:
So typically when I operate via cli I will use the gh cli to create a PR. CLAUDE.md from the repo root or ./claude/CLAUDE.md is loaded into context when working on a repo. If CLAUDE.md documents the skills / tools along with standards then I would hope an agent should be able to keep this in context.

If you want to keep the AGENTS.md then I propose you also add a `CLAUDE.md` (which is auto-loaded by claude with file contets:
```
@AGENTS.md
```

This just means import all context from AGENTS.md but triggered via claudes autoload of CLAUDE.md.

author: jtraglia
content: Yup. I wish there were a standard way of doing this. We're probably going to switch to a fully-claude oriented setup soonish.

author: taulepton_
content: However, maybe your skills direction eliminates the need for that if the skills definitions are loaded.

author: taulepton_
content: strong agree

author: taulepton_
content: AI vendor lock in is real 🙁

content: FYI we're going to try to follow this structure. Written by a coworker.

The skill set is a great idea to stop people like myself from opening PR's that trample on the great standards you have developed for the repo (sorry about that 😅 ).

author: taulepton_
content: Had you considered using pre-tool hooks to gate commands like “gh pr …” and “git commit …” to programmatically run the skills you define prior to the commands being executed. I think it’s a good fit https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
